root_cause_analyses.proximate_cause: Plugin loaded via importlib.import_module() with no sandboxing
root_cause_analyses.root_cause: Architectural flaw: Plugin system designed for extensibility without security isolation. Plugins run in same process with full host privileges. No defense-in-depth (no seccomp, no namespaces, no capability dropping, no network isolation).
contributing_factors: No plugin verification (no signatures, no hashes)
Trust model assumes all plugins are benign
execution_path: 1. Attacker creates malicious plugin with plugin.json
2. Plugin entry_point points to malicious module
4. Malicious module imports os, subprocess, etc.
5. Plugin executes arbitrary code on host
root_cause_analyses.impact: CRITICAL SECURITY VULNERABILITY: Arbitrary code execution on host. Attacker can steal API keys, access tenant data, deploy malicious code, pivot to other systems.

root_cause_analyses.proximate_cause: API key lookup via dict without constant-time comparison
root_cause_analyses.root_cause: Security anti-pattern: Using standard dict lookup for secret comparison. Python dict lookups have O(1) average but timing varies based on hash collisions and key presence. Attacker can measure response times to enumerate valid API keys.
contributing_factors: No rate limiting on API key validation
No logging of failed auth attempts
No account lockout after repeated failures
API keys passed in headers (potential log exposure)
execution_path: 1. Attacker sends request with guessed API key
3. Measure response time (faster = key not in dict)
4. Repeat with many keys, build timing profile
6. Use valid key to access tenant resources
root_cause_analyses.impact: HIGH SECURITY VULNERABILITY: API key enumeration leads to tenant data access, quota bypass, resource theft.

root_cause_analyses.proximate_cause: Auto-deploy allowed for high-confidence fixes without verification
root_cause_analyses.root_cause: Trust model flaw: LLM confidence score used as sole criterion for auto-deploy. LLMs can be overconfident in incorrect assessments. No code signing, no hash verification, no rollback testing, no human review for critical changes.
contributing_factors: Confidence threshold (0.9) arbitrary, not empirically validated
No code signing or integrity verification
No human review for sensitive operations
execution_path: 1. Attacker crafts issue that triggers high-confidence fix
2. LLM generates malicious fix with confidence >= 0.9
root_cause_analyses.impact: HIGH SECURITY VULNERABILITY: Unauthorized code deployment to production. Attacker can deploy backdoors, data exfiltration, ransomware.
